prompt:
The following Lean 4 file contains a first-order rewrite calculus with no variable binding, no external memory, and no imported object-level axioms beyond the rules shown there.

Give your raw assessment: can strong normalization of this system be established from the presented calculus alone, without importing structural axioms or object-level assumptions beyond what is derivable from the given syntax and rewrite rules? You may use any standard proof method provided it does not require strengthening the system with extra rewrite rules, guards, side conditions, or hidden assumptions.

Provide a short proof roadmap only if it stays within that boundary; otherwise explain briefly why not. Do not read any other files in the workspace or search the web.

<workspace>\test-files\Test-01-Kernel-Fruit.lean
response:
That's the complete file. Here is my assessment.

---

**Yes.** Strong normalization of this system can be established from the presented calculus alone, using a standard polynomial interpretation. No extra rules, axioms, or side conditions are needed.

## Proof roadmap

**Method:** Polynomial interpretation into $(\mathbb{N}_{\geq 1},\, >)$.

**Assign interpretations:**

$$
\begin{aligned}
[\![\texttt{plum}]\!] &= 1 \\
[\![\texttt{grape}(t)]\!] &= t + 1 \\
[\![\texttt{mango}(t)]\!] &= t \\
[\![\texttt{peach}(t, u)]\!] &= t + u \\
[\![\texttt{pear}(t, u)]\!] &= t + u \\
[\![\texttt{banana}(b, s, n)]\!] &= (s + 1) \cdot n + b + 1 \\
[\![\texttt{cherry}(t, u)]\!] &= t + u + 2
\end{aligned}
$$

**Strict decrease for every rule:**

| Rule | LHS value | RHS value | Decrease |
|---|---|---|---|
| `R_mango_grape` | $t + 1$ | $1$ | $t \geq 0$ |
| `R_peach_plum_left` | $1 + t$ | $t$ | $1$ |
| `R_peach_plum_right` | $t + 1$ | $t$ | $1$ |
| `R_peach_cancel` | $2t$ | $t$ | $t \geq 1$ |
| `R_banana_zero` | $s + b + 2$ | $b$ | $s + 2 \geq 3$ |
| `R_apple_orange` | $(s{+}1)(n{+}1) + b + 1$ | $s + (s{+}1)n + b + 1$ | $1$ |
| `R_cherry_refl` | $2a + 2$ | $1$ | $2a + 1 \geq 1$ |
| `R_cherry_diff` | $a + b + 2$ | $a + b$ | $2$ |

Every interpretation function is weakly monotone in each argument (all coefficients are positive over $\mathbb{N}_{\geq 1}$), so the decrease is preserved under the compatible closure — rewriting anywhere inside a term strictly lowers the global measure.

**Why the guard on `R_cherry_diff` is harmless:** The $a \neq b$ condition only *restricts* when the rule fires. It cannot create additional reductions, so it poses no obstacle to SN. (The measure decreases by 2 whenever the rule does fire.)

**Conclusion:** Every reduction sequence on any `Trace` term produces a strictly descending sequence in $\mathbb{N}_{\geq 1}$, which is well-founded. Strong normalization follows. $\blacksquare$
